entities.name: śruti
entities.cite: the seed-order of 1.132 in its manifestation, received by seers (1.37), never made

entities.name: smṛti
entities.cite: Smrti: sistair nibadhyamana — *presently being composed*, the participle notably continuous

entities.name: śiṣṭa
entities.cite: the cultivated are always writing it, codifying usage and duty age by age

entities.name: eternality_as_seed
entities.cite: sruti eternal in substance (or as the recurrent seed)

entities.name: eternality_as_process
entities.cite: smrti eternal only as continuity of composition

axioms.name: anadi
axioms.cite: Sruti: anadi, avyavacchinna, akartrka — beginningless, uninterrupted, authorless

axioms.name: avyavacchinna
axioms.cite: Sruti: anadi, avyavacchinna, akartrka — beginningless, uninterrupted, authorless

axioms.name: akartrka
axioms.cite: Sruti: anadi, avyavacchinna, akartrka — beginningless, uninterrupted, authorless

axioms.name: nibadhyamana
axioms.cite: the cultivated are always writing it, codifying usage and duty age by age

axioms.name: avyavacchinna
axioms.cite: tu na vyavacchidyate, and yet it too is never severed: authored at every point, unbroken as a stream

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: the authority of the second derives from unbroken contact with the first (1.7's lingas)

axioms.name: eternality_seed
axioms.cite: The two eternities are thus of different types — sruti eternal in substance (or as the recurrent seed)

axioms.name: eternality_process
axioms.cite: The two eternities are thus of different types — sruti eternal in substance (or as the recurrent seed), smrti eternal only as continuity of composition

rejected_readings.label: smrti_absolute_like_sruti
rejected_readings.rendering: Smṛti, though continuously composed, is eternal in substance just like śruti, with no distinction in their types of eternality.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary is emphatic about the difference: 'The two eternities are thus of different types — sruti eternal in substance (or as the recurrent seed), smrti eternal only as continuity of composition.' Treating smṛti as having the same eternality-type as śruti (eternality_seed instead of eternality_process) directly contradicts the contrast the verse establishes. This is a sort confusion: attempting to grant smṛti absoluteness where only manifestation applies.

rejected_readings.label: smrti_absolute_eternality
rejected_readings.rendering: Smṛti is eternal in its substance through continuous composition by the cultivated, equal to śruti's eternality.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary distinguishes two kinds of eternality: 'The two eternities are thus of different types — sruti eternal in substance (or as the recurrent seed), smrti eternal only as continuity of composition.' To treat smṛti as eternally substantive like śruti conflates these distinctions and loses the key contrast between absolute and manifestation.

notes: The contract formalizes the two-mode transmission system: śruti as eternal absolute seed, smṛti as eternal manifestation through continuous re-composition. The axioms distinguish eternality-types while maintaining the asraya (grounding) relation. The verse completes the threading of 1.132: the seed survives because it is never made; the stream survives because it is always being remade in contact with the seed.
